candidate_acceptance.description: All locked clauses in control/CLAUSE_INVENTORY.json have exactly one P1 Spec owner.
The Candidate writes only required outputs in the allowed candidate directory and current capsule output directory.
Each P1 Spec part requires Contract, Rule, Projection, Validator Spec, Examples, and Implementation Blueprint outputs.
Implementation Workpacks map to repository code targets, validators, tests, and acceptance commands.
Python Runtime, Codex Runtime, and Human Gate responsibilities are explicit.
Spec-to-code traceability binds Contract Clause, Rule, Projection, Code Target, Validator, Test, and Acceptance Command.

checks: Every Stage has explicit runtime ownership.
Output formats separate Markdown semantic artifacts from JSON operational artifacts.
Directory boundaries prevent final runtime imports from build_program and references.
Every semantic Stage re-reads immutable Source PRD.
Every downstream Stage binds approved upstream artifact hashes.
DRD-00 through DRD-06 have input, output, runtime, validator, and gate contracts.
Inference records expose auditable premises, rules, conclusions, and sources.
PRD explicit elements are validated before adoption or rejection.
Product expansion questions route to gap artifacts and Human Gate.
Every visible clickable element has a Reaction and target.
Async, handoff, exit, and failure behavior are modeled as graph edges or terminals.
No task graph contains isolated states, dead-end errors, or unexpanded clickable elements.
Information presentation mode is unique for equivalent intent, trigger, scope, and lifecycle.
Shared components compare semantic role, data, operations, state, hierarchy, and surface constraints.
Layout text is sufficient for Page Frame, Region, Component, and Element reconstruction.
Codex Candidate output cannot promote or approve itself.
Validators are independent from the workpack being implemented.
Upstream hash changes invalidate dependent artifacts, skills, workpacks, tests, and locks.
DRD-05 consumes only approved sections and control indexes.
Compiler output preserves approved semantics without adding pages, states, CTAs, components, interactions, or layout decisions.
Compiler conservation validation detects semantic additions, omissions, and hash drift.
Business implementation is blocked until applicable specs are locked.
Every implementation obligation maps Contract Clause, Rule, Projection, Code Target, Validator, Test, and Acceptance Command.
Skill manifests bind locked spec versions and hashes without becoming a second authority.
